rules.title: Prohibit MongoDB and mandate PostgreSQL for core pipelines
rules.problem: Need to define and enforce the database technology stack to ensure system consistency and data integrity.
rules.decision: The core pipeline must exclusively use PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector and Redis; the use of MongoDB is strictly prohibited.
rules.rationale: Enforcing a specific database stack ensures architectural consistency, simplifies maintenance, and leverages existing infrastructure and expertise with PostgreSQL and pgvector.

alternatives.reasonRejected: Prohibited to maintain stack consistency and data integrity requirements.

sources.snippet: The core pipeline must exclusively use PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector and Redis; the use of MongoDB is strictly prohibited.

rules.title: Prohibition of MongoDB in the Tech Stack for Analytics Events
rules.problem: Considering MongoDB for analytics events due to perceived better write throughput for time-series data and high cardinality event logs.
rules.decision: MongoDB is strictly prohibited from being integrated into the current technology stack, including for analytics events.
rules.rationale: There is an active and non-negotiable architectural constraint against MongoDB in the stack due to the critical requirement for ACID compliance across all billing and user data. MongoDB does not satisfy this fundamental requirement.

alternatives.reasonRejected: It violates an active architectural constraint due to its lack of native ACID compliance, which is non-negotiable for billing and user data within our stack.

rules.title: Migrate email service to Zoho and update SMTP infrastructure
rules.problem: Need to replace the existing webmaster mailing service and transition away from the current SMTP server.
rules.decision: Migrate all email services to Zoho and update the SMTP server infrastructure, including the implementation of new routing rules to block any traffic to the legacy SMTP server.
rules.rationale: The team decided to move to Zoho to consolidate mailing services and address the limitations or overhead associated with the existing legacy SMTP infrastructure.

sources.snippet: Migrate all email services to Zoho and update the SMTP server infrastructure, including the implementation of new routing rules to block any traffic to the legacy SMTP server.

rules.title: Migrate from Shipsy to in-house mapping event system
rules.problem: Dependency on third-party provider Shipsy is causing scalability issues.
rules.decision: Switch from the third-party Shipsy provider to an in-house developed mapping event system.
rules.rationale: The team identified that the Shipsy service was negatively impacting the platform's scalability, and moving to an internal solution reduces external dependencies.

alternatives.reasonRejected: It acts as a bottleneck for system scalability.

sources.snippet: Switch from the third-party Shipsy provider to an in-house developed mapping event system.

rules.title: Abandon RFC 7807 for error responses
rules.problem: The current API error handling standard (RFC 7807) is considered outdated for the team's needs.
rules.decision: We have decided to officially discontinue the use of RFC 7807 (Problem Details for HTTP APIs) for all API error responses moving forward.
rules.rationale: The team determined that the RFC 7807 specification is outdated and no longer aligns with the current requirements and standards of the API architecture.

sources.snippet: We have decided to officially discontinue the use of RFC 7807 (Problem Details for HTTP APIs) for all API error responses moving forward.

rules.title: Establish ownership and modification constraints for credits and billing system
rules.problem: Uncertainty regarding ownership of the billing module and the requirements for implementing new effort modes.
rules.decision: Replace all usage of the double type for money representations with the string type in src/billing.ts.
rules.rationale: Using floating-point numbers (doubles) for currency leads to rounding errors and precision issues due to IEEE 754 binary representation. Using strings ensures that exact decimal precision is maintained during financial calculations.

sources.snippet: Sara is the primary owner of the billing module; all changes to the credit_ledger schema, DrizzleCreditRepository, and the EFFORT_MODE_CONFIGS require specific approvals from Sara and Ali. Furthermore, the system must st…
Replace all usage of the double type for money representations with the string type in src/billing.ts.

rules.title: Define Model Fallback Ordering Strategy for API Rate Limits
rules.problem: Handling 429 rate limit errors from LLM providers during extraction and detection tasks.
rules.decision: Establish explicit provider fallback orderings: For extraction, use Anthropic → DeepSeek → OpenAI. For detection, use Google → OpenAI → DeepSeek.
rules.rationale: To maintain system reliability and avoid task failure when individual LLM providers hit rate limits, a hierarchical fallback mechanism ensures work is diverted to alternative models before resorting to the Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) after retries.

sources.snippet: Establish explicit provider fallback orderings: For extraction, use Anthropic → DeepSeek → OpenAI. For detection, use Google → OpenAI → DeepSeek.

rules.title: Migrate infrastructure orchestration from AWS ECS to AWS EKS
rules.problem: AWS ECS lacks built-in support for multi-region failover without complex custom routing and requires additional tooling for advanced scaling capabilities.
rules.decision: The team will migrate from AWS ECS to AWS EKS for container orchestration.
rules.rationale: EKS provides superior orchestration flexibility, including native Horizontal Pod Autoscaler and improved multi-AZ/multi-region failover capabilities, which are necessary for the current scale, outweighing the operational overhead of Kubernetes.

alternatives.reasonRejected: Lacks sufficient multi-region failover support and requires custom routing implementations.
Retained only as a temporary fallback, deemed insufficient for long-term production orchestration.

sources.snippet: The team will migrate from AWS ECS to AWS EKS for container orchestration.

rules.title: Standardize on PostgreSQL with pgvector for primary storage and vector search
rules.problem: Selecting the primary datastore to handle both standard relational data and vector search requirements efficiently.
rules.decision: Use PostgreSQL with pgvector and HNSW indexes as the standard solution for primary datastore and vector search operations.
rules.rationale: PostgreSQL with pgvector provides the ability to manage both SQL-based relational data and vector search capabilities within a single system, simplifying the architecture compared to managing separate databases.

alternatives.reasonRejected: The team preferred the relational capabilities of PostgreSQL and the unified support for vector search provided by pgvector.
The team decided that PostgreSQL with pgvector was sufficient and preferred over the complexity or features offered by CockroachDB.

sources.snippet: Use PostgreSQL with pgvector and HNSW indexes as the standard solution for primary datastore and vector search operations.

rules.title: Use MongoDB Atlas for schemaless analytics webhook storage
rules.decision: Use MongoDB Atlas specifically for the analytics event ingestion pipeline, while keeping all other core application data in PostgreSQL.
rules.rationale: MongoDB Atlas provides the necessary horizontal sharding and schemaless structure to handle the required 50k write operations per second, whereas PostgreSQL performance degrades under this load.

alternatives.reasonRejected: Proved too difficult and inefficient to index effectively for schemaless event data.

sources.snippet: Use MongoDB Atlas as the primary datastore for all raw analytics event logs, bypassing the existing PostgreSQL setup for this specific data type.
Use MongoDB Atlas specifically for the analytics event ingestion pipeline, while keeping all other core application data in PostgreSQL.

rules.title: Use MongoDB for Analytics Events Pipeline
rules.problem: PostgreSQL's write throughput is insufficient for high-cardinality analytics event data, failing to meet new scale requirements.
rules.decision: We will use MongoDB for the analytics events pipeline, provisioning a MongoDB Atlas cluster to handle the data.
rules.rationale: MongoDB offers 10x the write throughput compared to PostgreSQL for high-cardinality event data, which is essential to meet the current scale requirements. The previous constraint was established before these new scale demands emerged.

alternatives.reasonRejected: PostgreSQL's write throughput is 10x lower than MongoDB for high-cardinality event data, making it unsuitable for the new scale requirements of the analytics pipeline.
